text:
CT ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST AND CT CHEST WITHOUT CONTRAST
SURVEILLANCE IMAGING — ROUTINE ONCOLOGIC FOLLOW-UP

INDICATION:
Marguerite Okonkwo is a 48-year-old female with a history of bilateral high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma, FIGO Stage IIIC (pT3cN1M0), in the setting of a germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant (c.5266dupC). She underwent successful primary debulking surgery with R0 resection (complete gross resection), followed by six cycles of adjuvant carboplatin/paclitaxel chemotherapy with CA-125 normalization and complete radiographic response at end of treatment. She is currently approximately 6 months into first-line PARP inhibitor maintenance therapy with niraparib 100 mg daily (dose reduced from 200 mg due to prior grade 2 thrombocytopenia). This study is obtained as part of routine surveillance imaging to assess for disease recurrence. Most recent CA-125: 8 U/mL.

TECHNIQUE:
CT of the chest was performed without intravenous contrast using low-dose technique with multiplanar reformations. CT of the abdomen and pelvis was performed following the intravenous administration of 100 mL of iohexol contrast material (Omnipaque 350) with imaging obtained in the portal venous phase. Multiplanar reformatted images were reviewed in addition to axial source images. Oral contrast was not administered. No prior allergic reaction to contrast media on file.

COMPARISON:
Prior CT abdomen/pelvis with contrast and CT chest, end-of-chemotherapy restaging (post-cycle 6 carboplatin/paclitaxel). CT chest/abdomen/pelvis at initial staging.

---

FINDINGS:

CT CHEST:

Lungs and Airways:
The lungs are well aerated bilaterally. The airways are patent to the subsegmental level. No pulmonary nodules or masses are identified. No focal consolidation, ground-glass opacification, or air space disease. No interstitial abnormality. No evidence of lymphangitic carcinomatosis. The lung parenchyma is otherwise unremarkable.

Pleural Spaces:
No pleural effusion bilaterally. No pneumothorax. No pleural nodularity or thickening suggestive of pleural involvement.

Mediastinum and Hila:
The mediastinal and hilar contours are within normal limits. No pathologically enlarged mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes. The prevascular, paratracheal, subcarinal, and aortopulmonary window lymph node stations are all within normal size limits. Thyroid gland is not fully imaged but the visualized portions appear unremarkable.

Heart and Pericardium:
Heart is normal in size. No pericardial effusion. The aorta and great vessels are unremarkable in caliber without evidence of aneurysmal dilatation.

Chest Wall and Bony Thorax:
No destructive or sclerotic osseous lesions in the thoracic spine, ribs, or sternum. The visualized bones are intact.

---

CT ABDOMEN AND PELVIS:

Liver:
The liver is normal in size with a smooth contour. The hepatic parenchyma enhances homogeneously without focal hypo- or hypervascular lesions. No biliary ductal dilatation. The portal vein is patent with normal hepatopetal flow characteristics on portal venous phase imaging. No perihepatic free fluid. No capsular irregularity or subcapsular nodularity to suggest surface implants. The hepatic parenchyma appears normal.

Gallbladder and Biliary System:
Gallbladder is present with no cholelithiasis or wall thickening. No biliary ductal dilatation. Common bile duct is within normal caliber.

Spleen:
The spleen is normal in size and homogeneous in attenuation without focal lesions. No perisplenic fluid or nodularity along the splenic surface.

Pancreas:
The pancreatic parenchyma is normal in size and contour with uniform enhancement. Pancreatic duct not dilated. No peripancreatic fluid or soft tissue stranding.

Adrenal Glands:
Bilateral adrenal glands are normal in morphology and attenuation. No adrenal nodules or masses.

Kidneys and Ureters:
Bilateral kidneys enhance symmetrically without focal cortical defects, masses, or hydronephrosis. No renal calculi. No urothelial thickening or filling defects. The visualized ureters are not dilated.

Stomach and Small Bowel:
The stomach is normal in wall thickness without mucosal irregularity. Small bowel loops are normal in caliber and wall thickness throughout their visible extent. No evidence of obstruction, mesenteric stranding, or serosal nodularity along the small bowel loops.

Colon and Rectum:
The colon is normal in caliber. Colonic wall thickness is within normal limits. The sigmoid colon and its mesentery — a site of prior peritoneal implant resection — show expected post-surgical fibrous change without new nodular soft tissue thickening or serosal irregularity. The rectum is unremarkable.

Appendix:
The appendix is surgically absent, consistent with prior appendectomy at time of debulking surgery. Post-surgical changes in the right lower quadrant are stable and expected.

Peritoneum and Omentum:
The peritoneal surfaces are smooth throughout the visualized abdomen and pelvis. There is no peritoneal nodularity, implants, or soft tissue thickening along the parietal or visceral peritoneum. The right and left paracolic gutters are clear, without soft tissue deposits or free fluid. The mesenteric fat planes are preserved and clear. Critically, the region of the greater omentum — which was surgically resected in its entirety at the time of primary debulking (radical omentectomy) — shows expected post-surgical fatty change and fibrous residua without nodular soft tissue density or new omental mass formation. No ascites. No free fluid identified anywhere in the abdomen or pelvis.

Retroperitoneum and Lymph Nodes:
No pathologically enlarged retroperitoneal lymph nodes are identified. The para-aortic and paracaval lymph node chains at the infrarenal and suprarenal levels — sites of prior positive lymph node involvement and surgical dissection — demonstrate small, flat, and fatty-replaced lymph nodes within normal size limits. No discrete lymph node measuring above 1 cm in short axis. No pelvic lymphadenopathy. The external and internal iliac, obturator, and common iliac nodal stations are all unremarkable.

Pelvis:
The uterus and bilateral ovaries/fallopian tubes are absent, consistent with the patient's prior total abdominal hysterectomy and bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy. Post-surgical changes in the pelvis include expected soft tissue stranding and fine fibrous adhesions along the pelvic sidewalls and cul-de-sac — appearances that are stable and unchanged from the prior post-treatment study. The vaginal cuff appears normal in thickness without suspicious enhancement or nodularity. The urinary bladder is partially distended with normal wall thickness and no intraluminal filling defects. The rectosigmoid junction and parametrial soft tissues are unremarkable.

Diaphragm:
The right hemidiaphragm — site of prior peritoneal stripping — demonstrates expected post-procedural thickening and fibrotic change in the right subphrenic region, stable compared with prior imaging. No new soft tissue nodularity or pleural thickening at this site. No subphrenic fluid collection. The left hemidiaphragm is normal in contour.

Abdominal Wall and Musculature:
There is a well-healed midline laparotomy scar with expected post-surgical soft tissue changes and trace linear fibrosis in the anterior abdominal wall along the prior incision site. No incisional hernia. No soft tissue masses within the abdominal wall.

Bony Structures:
The lumbar spine demonstrates mild facet degenerative changes appropriate for age. No lytic or blastic osseous lesions in the visualized spine, sacrum, iliac wings, or proximal femora. No pathologic fractures.

Vascular Structures:
The abdominal aorta is normal in caliber. The inferior vena cava is patent without intraluminal filling defect. The mesenteric and portal vasculature is patent.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. No evidence of recurrent or residual high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma. The abdomen and pelvis are clear of peritoneal implants, omental nodularity, ascites, or soft tissue masses at any of the prior sites of disease.

2. No hepatic parenchymal lesions, no splenic implants, no adrenal involvement.

3. No pathologically enlarged pelvic, retroperitoneal, or para-aortic lymph nodes. The previously involved right external iliac and infrarenal para-aortic nodal stations are normalized in size, stable since completion of chemotherapy.

4. The right subphrenic soft tissue changes are stable post-procedural in character — no evidence of diaphragmatic recurrence at the prior peritoneal stripping site.

5. Post-surgical pelvis without evidence of vaginal cuff recurrence or new pelvic soft tissue lesion.

6. CT chest: No pulmonary nodules, no pleural effusion, no mediastinal or hilar adenopathy.

Overall, these findings represent a maintained complete response to prior therapy. There is no imaging evidence of disease recurrence in the setting of ongoing niraparib maintenance. The study is reassuringly negative across all previously involved anatomic compartments.
